title: Production of 10000x  TO-DMSO DNA gel stain
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"aj6id","text":"DNA dyes stain deoxyribonucleic acid for laboratory purposes such as detection and quantification. Many DNA dyes also bind to RNA and could be more broadly described as nucleic acid stains. Common dyes included ethidium bromide (EtBr), especially for agarose gel electrophoresis of DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":211,"length":16},{"key":1,"offset":251,"length":27}],"data":[]},{"key":"b0jl5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"395ct","text":"DNA gel electrophoresis using agarose is a common tool in molecular biology laboratories, allowing separation of DNA fragments by size. After separation, DNA is visualized by staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fmhgc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9vock","text":"Thiazole orange compares favorably to common staining methods in that it is sensitive, inexpensive, excitable with UV or blue light (to prevent sample damage), and safer than ethidium bromide.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4a0en","text":"Thiazole orange is known to interact with DNA and produce significant fluorescence which can be used to visualize DNA in electrophoresis gels.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"g3vk","text":"Thiazole orange powder can dissolve completely without precipitating in several solvents except water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"798ji","text":" guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bscra","text":"It is advisable to prepare the DNA gel stain in large batches(10-15 tubes) to enable easy weighing of the powder especially in situations where a high precision balance is unavailable.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Use a weighing balance to carefully measure 0.013 g of Thiazole Orange powder (Cas # ) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) (CAS: 107091-89-4) into a 1.5ml Eppendorf tube or opaque screw cap tube.
Use a micropipette to pipette 1000 µL of Dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) Contributed by users (CAS: 67-68-5) into the same Eppendorf tube.

Mix by gently inverting the tube several times to have a 10000x stock. This step may take a minute or less. 00:01:00 For immediate or daily use, leave at room temperature away from contact with sunlight (It can be stable up to 3 months. If not immediately used, store the gel stain stock in the fridge or freeze for 6 to 12 months. To prepare more than 1ml gel stain (say 10-15ml), calculate the amount of Thiazole orange powder needed for the final volume of gel stain desired. Weigh the powder and pour into a 50ml beaker, measure DMSO and add to the powder. Swirl gently for 1min to completely dissolve the powder. Transfer the gel stain into desired storage tubes by pipetting the desired volumes for each tube. DNA Electrophoresis Using Thiazole Orange Instead of Ethidium Bromide or Alternative Dyes.. Journal of visualized experiments : JoVE.

Follow this step to prepare a 1X TBE buffer from a 10X stock Prepare a 2% agarose gel by diluting 0.5g of agarose powder in 25ml 1x TBE or (calculate the corresponding amount of agarose to use for the percentage and volume of agarose gel desired). From the 10000x DNA gel stain stock, pipette 2.5 µL into 25 mL molten agar Cast the gel and leave to polymerize on the working bench at Room temperature . This may take about 00:20:00 . Load and run the gel immediately or you can keep the gel on the bench at room temperature till you are ready ( the gel can stay up to 3 to 6 hours without affecting visualization of DNA bands).
The following quality control tests are performed after producing the 10000x DNA gel stain to confirm it meets its desired specifications.

The functionality test is carried out to ensure the gel stain is able to allow visualization of amplified DNA templates after an agarose gel electrophoresis. This is done by performing gel agarose electrophoresis of any desired DNA template. Our lab adopted the Lambda genome using the 0.5 and 1kb regions for this test. This is done in 3 steps: Amplification of DNA template in a PCR reaction Running of Agarose gel electrophoresis Visualization of gel

Amplification of DNA template in a PCR reaction Thaw all reagents on ice in a bowl Label reaction tubes (PCR tubes) according to the number of samples, and including controls in each run (negative and positive controls) as needed. If using a 10x cellular reagent enzyme- Rehydrate the enzyme with 30μl of sterile PCR grade water, flick tube and keep on ice. Enzyme Master mixes can be used if available. In our case we used OpenVent DNA polymerase.

In each PCR tube, pipette and combine the following reagent components following the order in the table below (work on ice while pipetting) A B C D 1 Component Test Sample Negative Control 2 PCR Water Variable up to 20ul Variable up to 20ul 3 10x PCR buffer 2ul 2ul 4 10mM dNTP Mix 0.4ul 0.4ul 5 Forward/ Reverse primer 1/1 1/1 6 DNA template (0.5/1kb Lambda) 0.5-1ul / 7 DNA polymerase enzyme (OpenVent) 1ul 1ul
Gently mix the reaction tubes by flicking and transfer in the PCR machine to begin thermocycling.

Connect the thermocycler and program the protocol to run following the thermocycling steps listed below. The exact annealing temperature will depend on the choice of DNA template and primers used. A B C 1 Initial Denaturation 95°C 120secs 2 Denaturation 95°C 30sec 3 Annealing 50-65°C (depending on the length of the primers) 20secs 4 Extention 72°C Variable depending on primer 5 Final extention 72°C 120secs
The time of run will depend on the size of the DNA template and number of cycles of amplification (usually we use do 35 to 40 cycles).

Preparing and running agarose gel electrophoresis About 00:30:00 to the end of the thermocycling process, prepare a 1x TBE buffer from 10x stock following this protocol and use it to make a 2% agarose gel following this protocol . At this step a positive control gel a can be prepared using an EtBr based gel stain, which will be used to compare the results ( band quality) of the TO-DMSO gel stain. The two gels are then run under the same conditions and the resulting DNA bands visualized and compared.

Loading the agarose gel wells Pipette 3 to 5μl of DNA ladder (size depending on size of the DNA template) unto the first well then pipette the negative control carefully to avoid bubbles and load into the second well. Following the step as described here , mix your amplicons with DNA loading dye and load in the subsequent wells Allow the gel to run for 15-30mins at 48V on 0.8 or 2% agarose gel and TBE buffer

Visualization The amplification results are visualized by transferring the gel into a UV transilluminator or blue gel transilluminator With the help of the DNA ladder, determine if the amplification product is of the expected size and the bands are sharp and distinct looking exactly like those from the EtBr gel to show that the TO-DMSO gel stain was able to stain the DNA.

Determined by 1.5% TBE Agarose gel electrophoresis of 50 or100bp Ladder Follow the steps in preparing a 2% agarose gel and load as described in step 17 above. DNA templates or amplicon in this case is a 100bp DNA ladder. Run gel to finish and visualize the resulting band separation to confirm separation of the ladder into visible distinct bands .

Determined by 1.5% TBE Agarose gel electrophoresis of serial dilutions of 100bp Ladder Make 1:5 serial dilutions of the 100bp DNA ladder or any DNA amplicon and use the different dilutions as amplicons to load a gel well. Allow the gel to run through and visualize to determine the lowest concentration detectable by the gel stain. At this stage, the gel stain is ready to be used either on the spot or can be shipped or sent to an external lab and used following the steps described in the user protocol or instructions in step 4 above.
creator: Nadine Mowoh
